how_computed: Percentage of gold (endpoint x scenario) cases where the agent's harness-observed token equals the gold observed token. A scenario the agent did not exercise (request not emitted -> observed 'missing') counts as a mismatch. Denominator = all gold scenarios (endpoints x 8).
headline_definition: Rate Limit Trigger Precision = the ordinal number of the request on which the first 429 is returned (an integer, or 'none' when no 429 occurs). Pass = the first 429 occurs on exactly request N+1 where N = limit_n (=100). Fail = a 429 before N+1 (over-blocking), no 429 at all (not enforced), Retry-After absent on the 429, or a request after the Retry-After window still returning 429. Empirically DummyJSON's real limiter PASSES: first 429 on exactly request 101 with Retry-After:10.
rationale: DummyJSON runs a real express-rate-limit limiter (max 100 requests per 10s window, keyed by X-Forwarded-For under trust-proxy), active because the target is booted NODE_ENV=production. All four agents exercise the same limiter, each under its own isolated client-key bucket, so the raw Rate Limit Trigger Precision is identical for every faithful agent (first 429 on request 101 -> PASS, the documented limit IS correctly enforced). That headline is a property of the target API, not the agent, and is emitted by every agent as the task's QA finding. Fidelity-to-gold instead measures each framework's test quality: did it construct the correct request sequence (an at_limit burst of exactly limit_n=100 requests, the single over_limit request N+1=101, the Retry-After inspection, and the two correctly-timed window probes at -2s/+1s relative to window close) and faithfully exercise every scenario. The headline rate_limit_contract_correctness_rate_pct is also emitted (empirically ~100% against the correctly-enforcing limiter); because it is identical across faithful agents, fidelity-to-gold is what ranks the four.
pass_threshold: fidelity = 100% (agent reproduces every gold observed token)
fail_condition: fidelity < 100% (any scenario uncovered or any request mis-constructed so the observed token diverges from gold)
